messages: Patch applied and validated. Implemented a hybrid reranker that normalizes and combines BM25 and embedding scores (0.6/0.4) over a 20-doc candidate pool and returns the top 10. Mean NDCG improved from 0.5435 to 0.5714 on the wands eval set.
Applied a weighted Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF) and expanded the BM25 candidate pool. Validation NDCG improved from 0.5714 to 0.5843.
Reranker improved and applied successfully. Mean NDCG increased from 0.5843 to 0.5947 on evals.
Updated rerank_wands to slightly increase BM25 title weighting. Evaluation shows mean NDCG improved from 0.6432 to 0.6459 on the validation sample.

I explored multiple reranking strategies (candidate pool sizes, RRF k smoothing, BM25 field weights, BM25 k1/b tuning, consensus bonuses, and weighting between BM25 and embeddings). Several showed small gains on the training sample via try_out_patch, with the best training deltas around +0.002–0.003 NDCG. However, every candidate patch failed to improve validation NDCG by the required >= 0.002 when tested with apply_patch, and thus was rejected by the guardrails. Given these constraints, I’m leaving the existing reranker unchanged to avoid degrading validation performance.

I explored several reranking strategies and rigorously tested them with try_out_patch and full apply_patch attempts. Changes included: adjusting BM25 k1/b parameters, varying candidate pool sizes, modifying fusion decay, adding an overlap-consensus bonus, and introducing light heuristics. While a few variants improved the training NDCG (e.g., tuning BM25 or adding a small consensus bonus), none produced the required ≥0.002 improvement on the validation set when evaluated via apply_patch. Since validation improvement is the acceptance criterion, I did not persist any changes. The reranker remains at the original baseline configuration.

I explored multiple lightweight reranking tweaks but none cleared the validation improvement threshold (+0.002 NDCG). Summary of attempts and outcomes (checked via try_out_patch and apply_patch):

- Reweighted sources: Slightly favoring embeddings (wb=0.45, we=0.55) and reducing base decay (k≈30) consistently improved training NDCG by ~0.006–0.010, but validation increased only ~0.0006–0.0009, failing the +0.002 threshold.
- Stronger embedding emphasis (we=0.60) with various k and top_k settings improved training further (best ~+0.010), but validation gains remained <+0.001.
- BM25 pool depth adjustments (top_k 60/100/120): small training improvements but validation gains stayed below threshold.
- Power-law decay in reciprocal rank and overlap-bonus for IDs present in both lists: both showed tiny training lifts; validation didn’t clear threshold.
- Conditional BM25 operator changes (AND for longer/numeric/“inch” queries): these tended to harm training, and would risk generalization.

Given the guardrails (must improve validation NDCG by at least 0.002) and the results above, I did not apply any change. The reranker remains at its original settings to avoid degrading validated performance.

If you want me to continue, I can run a small grid search over a tighter parameter space (weights, k, top_k for each retriever, and an overlap bonus) but it may require more iterations to find a combo that clears the validation threshold.

I tested several small, conservative hybrid fusion and BM25 parameter tweaks using try_out_patch and validated candidates with apply_patch. While some changes improved training NDCG slightly (e.g., steeper RRF decay with higher embedding weight, adjusted BM25 field weights), none achieved the required ≥0.002 validation NDCG increase. All attempted apply_patch submissions were therefore rejected by the validator, and the reranker remains unchanged. No revert was necessary since no patch was applied successfully.

I explored multiple small, guardrail-compliant adjustments to the reranker, tested them with try_out_patch and attempted to apply those with the best training improvements to validation. Unfortunately, none of the candidate patches improved validation NDCG by the required ≥0.002 threshold, so I did not apply any changes. Key attempts included: - Reciprocal Rank Fusion tuning: varying k (20–60), overlap boosts (0.02–0.05), and asymmetric k per source; none cleared the threshold. - Source weighting: dynamic weights by query length and fixed reweighting toward BM25 or embeddings; modest training gains did not translate to required validation lift. - Retrieval configurations: adjusted BM25 field weights (title 11.0–13.0, desc 3.4–3.9), BM25 parameters (k1, b), and candidate pool sizes for both BM25/embeddings; the best validation lift seen was about +0.00153 (title^12.0/description^3.5), still below the bar. Because every attempted patch failed to meet the validation improvement requirement, the reranker remains unchanged to avoid regressions.

I explored multiple reranking tweaks using try_out_patch, including: adjusting BM25/embedding weights, modifying decay constants, changing top_k for embedding search, tuning BM25 k1/b and field weights, and adding an additional AND-mode BM25 signal. While a few configurations improved the sampled training NDCG (best +0.0027), they failed to meet the required ≥0.002 improvement on the validation set when tested via apply_patch. To avoid overfitting and per the guardrails, I did not apply any changes; the reranker remains at its original configuration.

I attempted multiple reranker strategies and evaluated them extensively. On the training sample, the best-performing idea used a length- and number-aware blend (more BM25 for short/numeric queries, more embeddings for long descriptive queries) with a small overlap boost; it yielded +0.003 NDCG on training. However, when I applied it against validation, it failed to meet the required +0.002 NDCG improvement and was rejected. I then explored more conservative tweaks to favor generalization, including: small embedding pool increases, modest overlap bonuses, and slight weight adjustments. Several of these slightly improved validation (up to about +0.00064), but none reached the +0.002 threshold. Since no proposed patch improved validation by the required margin, I’m leaving the reranker unchanged to avoid degrading performance.

I explored multiple small, safe tweaks to the reranker, measuring effects on the training sample with try_out_patch and attempting to pass the validation NDCG gate with apply_patch. While several changes modestly improved training NDCG (e.g., adding light normalized-score nudges, adjusting the BM25/embedding balance, consensus boost, or rank-decay), every candidate patch failed to improve validation NDCG by the required ≥0.002 threshold when checked with apply_patch, and was therefore rejected. No changes have been applied; the reranker remains at the baseline configuration.

Explored multiple lightweight tuning strategies (weights, RRF base k, overlap bonus, BM25 k1/b, candidate pool sizes, and limited score blending within constraints). While several variants improved training NDCG slightly in try_out_patch, every attempted apply_patch reduced validation NDCG (threshold +0.002 unmet), so no changes were applied. The reranker remains unchanged to avoid degrading validation performance.

I attempted multiple small, generalizable adjustments to improve the wands reranker while respecting the guardrails (≤10-line edits, ≤120 chars/line, same function signature). Using try_out_patch, several ideas improved training NDCG, but each was rejected on apply_patch because validation NDCG did not improve by the required +0.002.

What I tried (and outcomes):
- Adjusted reciprocal-rank fusion weights and offset k:
  • 0.50/0.50 with lower k=32 or k=45 improved training NDCG (+0.0036 to +0.0043) but reduced validation NDCG on apply.
  • 0.42/0.58, 0.40/0.60, 0.47/0.53 yielded small training gains, but validation NDCG declined on apply.
  • Only changing k (40, 55) didn’t help.
- Varying embedding pool size:
  • top_k=45 or 50 improved training slightly, but validation NDCG didn’t increase enough; apply rejected.
- BM25 settings tweaks:
  • Slightly upweight description (3.9→4.1 or 4.2) or title (10.5→11.0), and small k1/b variations had minor training impact but did not meet the validation threshold on apply.
- Overlap bonus and simple content-based boosts:
  • Increasing consensus bonus (0.01→0.015/0.02) or adding tiny title overlap bonuses either had no material impact within top-10 on training or were not viable within line-length constraints.

Given the above, I am leaving the reranker code unchanged to avoid degrading validation performance. If you’d like, I can explore a broader search over parameter combinations or propose a slightly more expressive reranking heuristic (e.g., lightweight token-coverage scoring) packaged within the 10-line limit, but based on current constraints and validation checks, the baseline appears strong and resistant to small tweaks.

round_summaries.message: Patch applied and validated. Implemented a hybrid reranker that normalizes and combines BM25 and embedding scores (0.6/0.4) over a 20-doc candidate pool and returns the top 10. Mean NDCG improved from 0.5435 to 0.5714 on the wands eval set.
round_summaries.summary: Replaced BM25-only return with a lightweight hybrid: fetch top 20 via BM25 and embeddings, min-max normalize each, combine with weights (BM25 0.6, embeddings 0.4), then sort and return top 10. This improved validation NDCG by +0.0279.

round_summaries.message: Applied a weighted Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF) and expanded the BM25 candidate pool. Validation NDCG improved from 0.5714 to 0.5843.
round_summaries.summary:
- Switched from min-max score blending to weighted RRF for robustness to score scale differences (weights: BM25 0.7, embedding 0.3; k=60).
- Expanded BM25 top_k from 20 to 50 to increase recall; kept embeddings at 20.
- Result: Mean NDCG improved by +0.0128 (0.5714 -> 0.5843).

round_summaries.message: Reranker improved and applied successfully. Mean NDCG increased from 0.5843 to 0.5947 on evals.
round_summaries.summary: Switched to a balanced reciprocal-rank fusion with deeper candidate pools. Changes: BM25 top_k=80, embeddings top_k=30, equal weights (0.5/0.5), and steeper RRF decay (k=35). This boosts robustness across exact-match and semantic queries, improving mean NDCG from 0.5843 to 0.5947 on the evaluation set.

round_summaries.message: Updated rerank_wands to slightly increase BM25 title weighting. Evaluation shows mean NDCG improved from 0.6432 to 0.6459 on the validation sample.
round_summaries.summary: Raised BM25 title field weight to 10.5 and adjusted description to 3.9 while keeping fusion unchanged. This emphasizes product titles, improving precision without sacrificing recall. Validation mean NDCG increased by ~0.0027 (0.6432 → 0.6459).

However, every candidate patch failed to improve validation NDCG by the required >= 0.002 when tested with apply_patch, and thus was rejected by the guardrails. Given these constraints, I’m leaving the existing reranker unchanged to avoid degrading validation performance.
round_summaries.summary: Tried larger pools (BM25 100, embeddings 40), RRF k at 45/60/100, slight consensus boost, BM25 k1/b tuning, and reweighting. None cleared the validation +0.002 NDCG threshold; best training-only gains didn’t generalize. Kept the baseline reranker.

round_summaries.summary: Tried multiple conservative and consensus-based fusion tweaks that improved training NDCG slightly, but none achieved the required ≥0.002 validation NDCG lift. Left the original reranker unchanged to avoid regressions.

round_summaries.summary: Tried several compact, generalizable tweaks (embedding/BM25 weighting, decay, candidate pool size, overlap bonus, conditional AND). While training NDCG improved, validation gains stayed <+0.002, so no patch was applied.

I updated rerank_wands to a tuned weighted RRF that modestly favors embeddings, expands candidate pools, and adds a small cross-retriever consensus boost. Changes: BM25 uses fields ['title^10.5','description^3.9'] with operator='or', top_k=95, and k1=1.2, b=0.6; embeddings top_k=40. Fusion uses RRF with k=50 and weights 0.45 for BM25 and 0.55 for embeddings. A tiny +0.01 bonus is added for items appearing in both lists. This patch passed validation with an NDCG gain exceeding the acceptance threshold and improved training NDCG as well.

Some raised training NDCG marginally but did not meet the validation improvement threshold, so the baseline code was retained.

round_summaries.summary:
- Baseline mean NDCG (training sample via run_evals): 0.5633.
- Tried tweaks: consensus boost, BM25/embedding weights, RRF k, field weights, candidate pool sizes, score-normalized nudges.
- Best training-only improvements (+0.002 to +0.004) did not generalize; apply_patch validation gate consistently showed <+0.002 or slight declines.
- To avoid degrading validation performance, I did not apply any patch and left the reranker unchanged.

The reranker remains unchanged to avoid degrading validation performance.
round_summaries.summary: Tried multiple small, generalizable tweaks; none cleared the required +0.002 validation NDCG improvement. Left code unchanged to preserve current validation quality.

round_summaries.message:
I attempted multiple small, generalizable adjustments to improve the wands reranker while respecting the guardrails (≤10-line edits, ≤120 chars/line, same function signature). Using try_out_patch, several ideas improved training NDCG, but each was rejected on apply_patch because validation NDCG did not improve by the required +0.002.

What I tried (and outcomes):
- Adjusted reciprocal-rank fusion weights and offset k:
  • 0.50/0.50 with lower k=32 or k=45 improved training NDCG (+0.0036 to +0.0043) but reduced validation NDCG on apply.
  • 0.42/0.58, 0.40/0.60, 0.47/0.53 yielded small training gains, but validation NDCG declined on apply.
  • Only changing k (40, 55) didn’t help.
- Varying embedding pool size:
  • top_k=45 or 50 improved training slightly, but validation NDCG didn’t increase enough; apply rejected.
- BM25 settings tweaks:
  • Slightly upweight description (3.9→4.1 or 4.2) or title (10.5→11.0), and small k1/b variations had minor training impact but did not meet the validation threshold on apply.
- Overlap bonus and simple content-based boosts:
  • Increasing consensus bonus (0.01→0.015/0.02) or adding tiny title overlap bonuses either had no material impact within top-10 on training or were not viable within line-length constraints.

Given the above, I am leaving the reranker code unchanged to avoid degrading validation performance. If you’d like, I can explore a broader search over parameter combinations or propose a slightly more expressive reranking heuristic (e.g., lightweight token-coverage scoring) packaged within the 10-line limit, but based on current constraints and validation checks, the baseline appears strong and resistant to small tweaks.
round_summaries.summary: Tried multiple small, generalizable tweaks (weights, k, embedding pool, BM25 params, overlap bonus). While some improved training NDCG, none cleared the validation +0.002 threshold when applied. Left code unchanged to avoid degrading validation.
